queue.task: Research: read a real database internals resource (Andy Pavlo lectures, CockroachDB design docs, or SQLite architecture)
queue.summary: Read SQLite architecture docs. Key insight: SQLite compiles to bytecode (VDBE), stores everything in B-Trees, and has massive query planner complexity. HenryDB needs: B-Tree organized tables for PK lookups, cost-based join optimizer, possible bytecode compilation.

queue.summary: Found that db.js uses inline NESTED_LOOP_JOIN, completely ignoring the HashJoin in volcano.js. This is why JOINs are 2.8s. Fix: wire db.js _select to use HashJoin when equi-join conditions detected. The code exists, its just not connected.

queue.task: Analyze db.js join execution path. Map exactly where nested loop happens and design hash join integration.
queue.summary: Mapped join path: db.js _executeJoin lines 1623-1637 does nested loop with full heap scan per left row. Need to detect equi-join conditions from join.on AST, build hash map on right table join column, probe for each left row. O(n*m) -> O(n+m). Filter condition: join.on is a BinaryExpression with = operator and column refs from different tables.

queue.goal: Wire hash join into db.js for equi-joins. Target: reduce JOIN benchmark from 2.8s to <100ms.

queue.task: Implement hash join in db.js _executeJoin: detect equi-join, build hash map, probe. Support INNER and LEFT JOIN.
queue.summary: Hash join implemented: detect equi-join from AST, build hash map on right table, probe with left rows. JOIN benchmark: 2798ms → 20.3ms (138x speedup). All 101 core tests passing. Zero regressions.

queue.task: Re-run benchmark, verify JOIN drops from 2.8s to <100ms. Run server tests to ensure no regressions.

queue.task: Analyze UPDATE/DELETE performance: 498ms and 1028ms for 1K rows. Where is the bottleneck?
queue.summary: UPDATE/DELETE bottleneck: individual WAL commit per row (200 rows = 200 txns). Also _valuesToRow called on all rows including non-matching. Fix: batch WAL into single txn, skip row conversion for non-matching.

queue.task: Optimize UPDATE/DELETE: avoid full WAL replay per row, batch operations
queue.summary: Batched WAL transactions for UPDATE/DELETE. UPDATE 498ms→17ms (29x), DELETE 1028ms→11ms (96x). Combined with hash join, all operations now <20ms for 1K rows. 32 core tests passing.

queue.task: Investigate why orders INSERT is 5x slower than users INSERT (1341ms vs 276ms for 1K rows each)
